title: "Issues setup higher resolutions"
date: 2013-01-25
forum: Multimedia Software

### Post by LaJuan on 2013-01-25
I'm trying to get my PC to use an higher resolution using a Acer P191w monitor. Currently running Ubuntu 12.04LTS. I open Nvidia Settings and get this error:

You do not appear to be using the NVIDIA X driver.  Please edit your X configuration file (just run `nvidia-xconfig` as root), and restart the X server.

I run this and here what my new file looks like:

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 304.64  (buildmeister@swio-display-x86-rhel47-01.nvidia.com)  Tue Oct 30 12:19:38 PDT 2012


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 33.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 72.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "NoLogo" "True"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

It looks like the card or computer doesn't even see the monitor in this file. I've been chasing my tail on this one. Can anybody shed some light so I can gain higher resolution?

Thanks,

LaJuan

---

### Post by Yellow Pasque on 2013-01-25
As I said in your previous thread ( [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2006187](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2006187) ), the nvidia 3xx driver doesn't support your card. You need to be using nvidia-173 package.

---

### Post by LaJuan on 2013-01-28
Thanks Temüjin!!!!!!!!!!!! Works like a champ now!!!!!!!!!!!!):P):P):P

---
